    Client Interest Key [+] Show Details...[-] Hide Details
     
    Interested:This listing was emailed by a Realtor to their client/prospect who added to it to their favorites list, assigning an interest level of "Interested"
    Maybe:This listing was emailed by a Realtor to their client/prospect who added to it to their favorites list, assigning an interest level of "Maybe"
    Unknown:This listing was emailed by a Realtor to their client who has either not yet viewed it or not yet assigned their interest level
    Not Interested:This listing was emailed by a Realtor to their client/prospect, who marked it "Not Interested"
    Not emailed:This listing matches the criteria of another Realtor's search. The search has not yet run, does not run automatically, or does not email matches to clients.

    Reverse Prospect Hints [+] Show Details...[-] Hide Details
    • "Allow Reverse Prospecting" must be assigned to clients: A Reverse Prospect search only considers searches for which Realtors have assigned the "Allow Reverse Prospecting" permission to the associated clients/prospects. These clients will be assigned a "Public ID" which is visible to the Realtors from their "My Clients" and "Prospecting Manager" lists.

    • Only Active Listings are considered: If you run a Reverse Prospect search against a listing from your My Listings/Transactions list in any status other than one of the Active statuses, it will not be considered by the Reverse Prospect search. Likewise, if you attempt to run a Reverse Prospect search for a listing from your My Listings/Transactions list for which you are the Buyer Broker, it too will be disregarded.

    • You may have to wait up to 24 hours to see results for a new listing: A Reverse Prospect search takes advantage of automated property searches that run every hour, on the hour, throughout the day. Reverse Prospect searches also take advantage of interest levels assigned by Realtors' clients/prospects reviewing property matches. As a result, you may have to wait up to 24 hours to see any reverse prospecting search results for a new listing.

    • Reverse prospect searches consider ALL property searches: Assuming a Realtor has assigned "Allow Reverse Prospecting" for their Active client/prospect, then Reverse Prospect search considers all saved/automated searches for that client, along with any property search results that were manually emailed to that client. A manually emailed search will have a blank search ID in your reverse prospect search results.

    • Sort your results by Client Interest and Dates for relevance: Click on the column headers to sort your reverse prospect search results to put the most recent or "Interested" prospects at the top of the list. These are likely to be the prospects whose agents in which you are most interested to contact.

    • Email Realtors from their Business Card in your results: To contact a Realtor in regard to a reverse prospect search, click on their name to display their business card. The Realtor will usually have an email on the business card which, when clicked, will bring up your email program. The client's public ID is conveniently added to the subject line. The Realtor who you are contacting can look up the Client's Public ID in their "Prospecting Manager" list or their "My Clients" list.

    • You can run a reverse prospect search against a Draft listing: If you are curious what sort of matches you might get after a price change or for an upcoming listing, you can run a reverse prospect search for a Draft listing. Click on the Status drop-down list on your My Listings/Transactions list to find your Drafts. You may want to use the "Copy/Clone" option when creating a draft listing for these purposes. IMPORTANT: A Draft listing will never be seen by Realtors' clients/prospects, therefore will never include their interest in your reverse prospect search results. Also, Draft listing reverse prospect searches are only effective after 5:00am the day after you create the Draft.
